title: Johnson & Johnson — Hematology — briefing Q4-2025
html:
What Changed in J&J Hematology — Q4 2025

Quarter: October–December 2025 Earnings reported: J&J January 22 | Sanofi January 2026 | BMS January 2026 | Pfizer January 2026 Generated: 2026-04-27

1. TL;DR

  1. DARZALEX hit $14.4B for FY2025 — the largest MM franchise in history, with Q4 at $3.9B (+24.1%) and 12-point frontline share gain.
  2. MajesTEC-3: TEC-DARA reduced progression/death risk by 83% in 2L — the most consequential MM clinical data this year; bispecifics move from rescue therapy to early intervention.
  3. BMS's Abecma slipped to ~$140M in Q4 — CAR-T market share is consolidating around CARVYKTI; BMS flagged manufacturing and T-cell malignancy concerns.
  4. Revlimid FY2025 dropped to ~$4.5B (from $6.7B in FY2024) — generic lenalidomide is reshaping MM economics; the backbone cost is collapsing.
  5. Pfizer's Elrexfio closed FY2025 at ~$200M — growing but structurally behind TECVAYLI due to lack of combination data.

2. J&J Financial Signals

Product Q4 2025 FY 2025 Q4 Growth
DARZALEX $3.9B $14.4B +24.1%
CARVYKTI ~$500M ~$2.0B +57%
TECVAYLI ~$200M ~$750M +31%
TALVEY ~$150M ~$550M +61%
Total MM ~$4.75B ~$17.7B —
  • MajesTEC-3 83% risk reduction in 2L is paradigm-level data — FDA filing expected imminently
  • J&J targeting $50B+ company revenue by decade-end; MM franchise is 35% of the way there
  • ASH 2025 was dominated by J&J: DARZALEX frontline, bispecific combos, CARVYKTI expansion

What J&J management said on the Q4 2025 call:

  1. "On track for $15B+ peak" for DARZALEX — management set the most aggressive peak sales guidance for any single oncology asset. At $14.4B FY2025, J&J is signaling the brand has meaningful growth ahead, driven by frontline share expansion and combination evolution.
  2. "DARZALEX will be our first $15B brand" — the milestone framing positions DARZALEX as a company-defining asset. This language is designed to anchor analyst models and raise consensus estimates.
  3. TEC-DARA (TECVAYLI + DARZALEX) positioned as complementary to CARVYKTI — when analysts raised cannibalization concerns, J&J's response was explicit: "Different patient populations, complementary not competitive." Management is proactively managing the internal portfolio narrative.
  4. $50B+ by decade-end aspiration reaffirmed — with the MM franchise at ~$17.7B annualized (35% of the target), hematology is positioned as the single largest contributor to J&J's long-range growth story.

Key analyst Q&A signals:

  1. Analysts asked directly about CARVYKTI vs. TEC-DARA cannibalization — the 2L portfolio management question was raised explicitly. J&J's "different patient populations" answer is the official position, but analysts will demand data to support it. ZoomRx's segmentation tracking becomes the evidence base.
  2. Manufacturing capacity was probed again — consistent with Q3, CARVYKTI manufacturing remains the #1 analyst concern. The question isn't whether physicians want CARVYKTI — it's whether J&J can make enough of it.
  3. No analyst questioned DARZALEX's $15B+ peak — the target went unchallenged, suggesting the Street views it as achievable given frontline share trajectory and combination data. Consensus appears aligned with management.

3. Competitor Earnings Intelligence

Revenue Snapshot — Q4 2025 / FY 2025

Company Brand Q4 2025 FY 2025 Key Signal
Sanofi Sarclisa ~$260M ~$950M Growing in combos; <7% of DARZALEX scale
BMS Abecma ~$140M ~$550M Declining; manufacturing + safety headwinds
BMS Revlimid ~$1.0B ~$4.5B Down 33% YoY; generics accelerating
Pfizer Elrexfio ~$55M ~$200M Growing 50%+ but tiny; no 2L combo data
AbbVie Imbruvica ~$650M ~$2.8B Down 25%; Brukinsa (BeiGene) taking CLL share

Sanofi — Sarclisa (~$260M Q4, ~$950M FY2025)

Sanofi FY2025 call (January 2026):

  1. Sarclisa approaching $1B but the ceiling is visible: Sanofi reported Sarclisa at ~$950M FY2025, growing ~18%. Management highlighted IKEMA long-term follow-up (PFS2 benefit in 2L+) and the IsKia Phase 3 trial in NDMM as the growth catalysts.

  2. Sanofi's 2L positioning undermined by MajesTEC-3: With TEC-DARA showing 83% risk reduction in 2L, Sanofi's Sarclisa-Kd 2L positioning looks increasingly obsolete. The 2L market is shifting from "anti-CD38 combo" to "bispecific + anti-CD38" — and TEC-DARA owns that space.

  3. IsKia is Sanofi's frontline play: The IsKia trial (Sarclisa-KRd in transplant-ineligible NDMM) is the only data package that could give Sarclisa a meaningful frontline position. But even if positive, it competes against DARZALEX-VRd (MAIA data) and DARZALEX-VRd (PERSEUS data) — both deeply established.

Implication for J&J: Sarclisa's growth is real but irrelevant to DARZALEX's franchise trajectory. The risk scenario is IsKia non-inferiority + payer pressure for a lower-cost anti-CD38. J&J's defense is DARZALEX SubQ (FASPRO) convenience + data breadth.

BMS — Abecma ($140M Q4) + Revlimid ($1.0B Q4, ~$4.5B FY)

BMS FY2025 call (January 2026):

  1. Abecma declining on multiple fronts: BMS reported Abecma at ~$550M FY2025, down from ~$650M in FY2024. Three headwinds converging: (a) CARVYKTI's stronger KarMMa-3 data and smoother manufacturing, (b) FDA's boxed warning on T-cell malignancies post-CAR-T (affecting all CAR-T but hitting Abecma harder due to existing safety concerns), (c) capacity constraints that BMS hasn't resolved.

  2. BMS signaling strategic retreat from MM: Management's language shifted from "competing for CAR-T leadership" to "optimizing Abecma within the broader oncology portfolio." BMS is investing in next-gen CAR-T (allogeneic, multi-target) and pivoting hematology focus to Breyanzi (LBCL) and Reblozyl (MDS/beta-thalassemia).

  3. Revlimid genericization accelerating: At ~$4.5B FY2025 (down 33%), Revlimid's decline is terminal. Generic lenalidomide is now >50% of new Revlimid-class scripts. This reshapes MM economics: the cost of len-backbone regimens drops by 60-70%, making premium therapies (bispecifics, CAR-T) relatively more cost-effective.

Implication for J&J: BMS's MM retreat is the biggest competitive tailwind in hematology. With Abecma declining and no new MM assets in late-stage development, J&J faces minimal external CAR-T competition through at least 2028. Revlimid genericization benefits J&J's DARA-Rd combinations (cheaper backbone) and creates payer headroom for expensive TEC-DARA and CARVYKTI.

Pfizer — Elrexfio (~$55M Q4, ~$200M FY2025)

Pfizer FY2025 call (January 2026):

  1. Elrexfio growing but structurally disadvantaged: At $200M FY2025 (~50% growth), Elrexfio is a viable bispecific but lacks the combination data that defines the 2L market. J&J's MajesTEC-3 (TEC-DARA 83% risk reduction) has no Pfizer equivalent.

  2. MagnetisMM-7 is Pfizer's hope: Phase 3 testing Elrexfio + DARZALEX (J&J's own molecule) in RRMM. The trial design is notable — Pfizer's best combination partner is J&J's DARZALEX, which means even if MagnetisMM-7 succeeds, the commercial value partially accrues to J&J.

  3. Pfizer's oncology pivot to ADCs and targeted: Pfizer's earnings call emphasized Padcev (bladder), Adcetris (lymphoma), and seribantumab pipeline. Elrexfio is not positioned as a strategic priority — it's a participating product, not a franchise-builder.

Implication for J&J: Elrexfio remains a monitoring item. MagnetisMM-7 readout (2026-2027) is the only data event that could change the dynamic — and even then, the combination includes DARZALEX. J&J's bispecific advantage is secure through 2027.

AbbVie — Imbruvica (~$650M Q4, ~$2.8B FY2025, -25%)

AbbVie FY2025 call (January 2026):

  1. Imbruvica in terminal decline: Down 25% as BeiGene's Brukinsa (zanubrutinib) captures CLL/SLL new starts. AbbVie acknowledged Brukinsa's ALPINE head-to-head superiority and is no longer investing in Imbruvica lifecycle extensions.

  2. J&J co-promote economics declining: As the co-promote partner, J&J receives a share of Imbruvica revenue. The decline reduces J&J's CLL/lymphoma revenue contribution — but this is a small line item relative to the MM franchise.

Implication for J&J: Imbruvica's decline is a known quantity. The CLL/lymphoma space is shifting to Brukinsa and fixed-duration Ven-Obi regimens. J&J's hematology future is 100% MM.

Synthesis: What the Combined Picture Tells Us

  1. J&J's hematology franchise faces minimal external competitive risk — Sarclisa ($950M) is niche, Abecma ($550M) is declining, Elrexfio ($200M) is small. Combined, they're <10% of J&J's $17.7B MM portfolio.

  2. BMS's strategic retreat from MM is a generational tailwind — Abecma declining + Revlimid going generic means BMS, which defined MM treatment for 20 years, is exiting the market. J&J inherits.

  3. Revlimid genericization is the hidden macro tailwind — cheaper backbone = more payer headroom for premium agents (DARZALEX, TEC-DARA, CARVYKTI). The total cost of MM treatment may actually DECREASE even as biologic utilization INCREASES.

  4. The competitive risk is internal — TEC-DARA (2L bispecific) vs. CARVYKTI (2L CAR-T) cannibalization is the real portfolio management challenge. External competitors are not the concern.

4. Clinical/Regulatory

  • MajesTEC-3: 83% risk reduction in 2L RRMM — FDA filing expected Q1 2026
  • MajesTEC-9: TECVAYLI monotherapy in first relapse — expanding bispecific evidence base
  • ASH 2025: J&J dominated — DARZALEX frontline, CARVYKTI expansion, bispecific combos
  • IsKia (Sanofi): Sarclisa-KRd in NDMM Phase 3 — the one external trial that matters
  • MagnetisMM-7 (Pfizer): Elrexfio + DARZALEX — Pfizer's combination play, but includes J&J's molecule

5. Thesis Update

Thesis Status Evidence This Quarter
1. TEC-DARA reshapes 2L STRONGLY CONFIRMED 83% risk reduction; FDA filing imminent; Sanofi's 2L Sarclisa positioning now obsolete
2. DARZALEX is the annuity CONFIRMED $14.4B FY, 24.1% Q4 growth, frontline domination; Sarclisa at <7% of scale
3. External competition declining CONFIRMED Abecma down, Revlimid going generic, Elrexfio small, BMS retreating from MM
4. Bispecific vs CAR-T sequencing STRENGTHENED MajesTEC-3 gives bispecifics 2L data; CARVYKTI also has 2L data; segmentation is the answer
5. TALVEY underappreciated UNCHANGED Growing but no new data events at ASH

6. What to Watch Next Quarter (Q1 2026)

  1. TEC-DARA FDA filing/approval timeline — expected Q1 2026
  2. CARVYKTI FY2025 final number — $2B+ confirms franchise status
  3. Abecma trajectory — continued decline cements CARVYKTI dominance
  4. IsKia interim data — any signal from Sanofi's frontline trial?
  5. ZoomRx heme MSL readout — overdue; flag with team

7. Conversation Starters for J&J

  1. "BMS is exiting MM — Abecma declining, Revlimid going generic, no new assets in late-stage. You're inheriting a $20B+ market with minimal external competition. The question is no longer 'can you win MM?' — it's 'how do you optimize a $20B franchise?' Portfolio sequencing research is the highest-value work we can do." — Reframes the competitive landscape.

  2. "Revlimid going generic drops the backbone cost 60-70%. That makes DARZALEX-Rd combinations look even more cost-effective and creates payer headroom for TEC-DARA and CARVYKTI. Have you modeled the economic impact? We can track payer formulary changes as a leading indicator." — Competitor-economics insight.

  3. "Pfizer's MagnetisMM-7 is testing Elrexfio + YOUR DARZALEX. If it succeeds, the commercial value partially accrues to you. This is the rare case where a competitor's success is also your success. We should be tracking this for dual-brand intelligence." — Novel competitive framing.

Sources: J&J Q4/FY2025 (Jan 22, 2026), Sanofi FY2025 (Jan 2026), BMS FY2025 (Jan 2026), Pfizer FY2025 (Jan 2026), AbbVie FY2025 (Jan 2026).

Back to TA Knowledge Hub | Home

Sanofi FY2025 call (January 2026):

BMS — Abecma ($140M Q4) + Revlimid ($1.0B Q4, ~$4.5B FY)
BMS FY2025 call (January 2026):

Revlimid genericization accelerating: At ~$4.5B FY2025 (down 33%), Revlimid's decline is terminal. Generic lenalidomide is now >50% of new Revlimid-class scripts. This reshapes MM economics: the cost of len-backbone regimens drops by 60-70%, making premium therapies (bispecifics, CAR-T) relatively more cost-effective.


Implication for J&J: BMS's MM retreat is the biggest competitive tailwind in hematology. With Abecma declining and no new MM assets in late-stage development, J&J faces minimal external CAR-T competition through at least 2028. Revlimid genericization benefits J&J's DARA-Rd combinations (cheaper backbone) and creates payer headroom for expensive TEC-DARA and CARVYKTI.

AbbVie — Imbruvica (~$650M Q4, ~$2.8B FY2025, -25%)
AbbVie FY2025 call (January 2026):

Implication for J&J: Imbruvica's decline is a known quantity. The CLL/lymphoma space is shifting to Brukinsa and fixed-duration Ven-Obi regimens. J&J's hematology future is 100% MM.

J&J's hematology franchise faces minimal external competitive risk — Sarclisa ($950M) is niche, Abecma ($550M) is declining, Elrexfio ($200M) is small. Combined, they're <10% of J&J's $17.7B MM portfolio.

MajesTEC-3: 83% risk reduction in 2L RRMM — FDA filing expected Q1 2026
MajesTEC-9: TECVAYLI monotherapy in first relapse — expanding bispecific evidence base
